title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership · Runtime Lens: Run Reset
summary: Menus, play, pause, death, and game over are booleans that form impossible combinations; pause leaves timers running; restart retains old projectiles and score. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the run reset increment in explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Reset run-scoped entities, timers, score, lives, seed, and transient effects without destroying user settings.
Freeze simulation clocks and dangerous input while keeping menus, status, audio control, and window events responsive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-game-state-scenes-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-game-state-scenes-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_game_state_scenes_2(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-run-reset-dg6ifx
# Competency: asteroids-run-reset.
# Case 6635: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_run_reset_dg6ifx(states: int = 6, valid_transitions: int = 9, invalid_transitions: int = 0, pause_freezes_simulation: bool = True, restart_resets_run: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dg6ifx = "dg6ifx-xfi6gd-6635"
    assert evidence_variant_dg6ifx
    evidence_axes_dg6ifx = (
        "playerdg6ifx",
        "seed6635",
        "inputxfi6gd",
        "framedg6ifx6635",
        "state6635dg6ifx",
        "playtestxfi6gd6635",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dg6ifx)) == 6
    if states < 4 or valid_transitions < states:
        return False, states, "state-graph"
    if invalid_transitions > 0:
        return False, invalid_transitions, "illegal-transition"
    if not pause_freezes_simulation:
        return False, valid_transitions, "pause-leak"
    if not restart_resets_run:
        return False, valid_transitions, "restart-leak"
    assert valid_transitions >= states
    return True, valid_transitions, "explicit-scenes"

options.text: For repair the run reset defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Run Reset, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the run reset defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Run Reset model · Theory Run Reset
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Run Reset: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 5282 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Reset run-scoped entities, timers, score, lives, seed, and transient effects without destroying user settings.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Run Reset model · Theory Run Reset.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Run Reset model · Theory Run Reset, keep Run Reset connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reset run-scoped entities, timers, score, lives, seed, and transient effects without destroying user settings.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Run Reset model · Theory Run Reset, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Run Reset precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Run Reset model · Theory Run Reset, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 5282 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Reset run-scoped entities, timers, score, lives, seed, and transient effects without destroying user settings.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Run Reset model · Theory Run Reset, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Recreating the player is sufficient restart cleanup.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-run-reset-dg6ifx(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_run_reset_dg6ifx\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?states < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?invalid_transitions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not pause_freezes_simulation)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not restart_resets_run)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
